calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Continued To Jul-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Continued To May-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Continued To Apr-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-89-116967 Estate of Arthur Oppenheimer & PES-89-131445 Estate of Martha Oppenheimer.
Appearances: Keith Bilter for (petitioner) Yuen Gin. Jack Johal (via court call) for (beneficiary) Betsy Bliss.

The Court stated that we are here on an interim and final accounting, that there has been civil litigation, that issues have been raised and asked Mr. Johal if he has objections. Mr. Johal stated yes, that they just received the supplemental accounting and that their objections are straightforward. The court stated that the objections need to be in writing, that the examiner's notes need to be addressed, that an amendment may be required, confirmed that the civil litigation went to trial and asked if the request for a stay was still pending. Mr. Bilter stated that he is not sure if the order has been signed yet, that the accounting issues were resolved in the litigation but that there are still issues regarding the fees. The Court stated that it will continue this matter to give the petitioner time to amend his petition and for the respondent to file objections. Mr. Bilter stated that he is in agreement but that he would like to do distribution as the markets are volatile. The Court stated that it is not in a position to do that without an accounting, that it may get there soon or that counsel might file a petition for preliminary distribution. Mr. Bilter stated that they would need two months as the accountants are in tax season now. The Court reminded counsel that he will need to re-serve. The accounting petitions in both case numbers were continued to April 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects the amended petition and objections to be filed prior to this date and that counsel meet and confer as to how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
Petition For Order Directing Succession Of Trustees
Continued From 6/29 To 7/11 On 5th & Final Rept, Waiver Of Acct, Removal Of Trust From Cont Court Jurisdiction
Fifth & Final Rept.; Waiver Of Accounting; Petn. For Removal Of Trust From Continuing Court Jurisdiction
